com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.led

Interface LED

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Module

    public interface LED
extends Module
    Provides the ability to operate the LED lights.

    How to get this module:

         LED led = (LED)NSDKModuleManagerImpl.getInstance().getModule(ModuleType.LED);

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void blink(LEDColor[] ledColor, int count, int timeInterval)
      Makes LED lights blink.
      void setState(LEDColor[] ledColor, LEDState ledState)
      Sets the state of specified LED lights.
      void setState(LEDLight[] lights)
      Sets the state of specified LED lights.

    • Method Detail

      • setState

        void setState(LEDColor[] ledColor,
              LEDState ledState)
       throws NSDKException
        Sets the state of specified LED lights.

        The light will keep the state until next setting.

        Example:

             try {
         // Turn on the green and blue lights.
         LEDColor[] lights = new LEDColor[]{LEDColor.GREEN, LEDColor.BLUE};
         led.setState(lights, LEDState.ON);

         // Turn off the yellow light.
         lights = new LEDColor[]{LEDColor.YELLOW};
         led.setState(lights, LEDState.OFF);

         // Make all the lights blink.
         lights = new LEDColor[]{LEDColor.GREEN, LEDColor.BLUE, LEDColor.YELLOW, LEDColor.RED};
         led.setState(lights, LEDState.BLINK);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception.
     }
        Parameters:
        ledColor - [Required] LED lights to control. See LEDColor
        ledState - [Required] The state of LED lights. See LEDState
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • setState

        void setState(LEDLight[] lights)
       throws NSDKException
        Sets the state of specified LED lights.

        Supported devices:

        • P300
        Parameters:
        lights - [Required] LED lights to control. See LEDLight
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • blink

        void blink(LEDColor[] ledColor,
           int count,
           int timeInterval)
    throws NSDKException
        Makes LED lights blink.

        The light will be turned off after blinking finished.

        Example:

             // Make the green and blue lights blink for 3 times every 1 second.
     LEDColor[] lights = new LEDColor[]{LEDColor.GREEN, LEDColor.BLUE};
     int count = 3;
     int timeInterval = 1000;
     try {
         led.blink(lights, count, timeInterval);
     } catch (NSDKException e) {
         // Handle the exception.
     }
        Parameters:
        ledColor - [Required] LED lights to control. See LEDColor
        count - [Required] How many times to blink the lights, shall be >0.
        timeInterval - [Required] Time interval(units: ms), shall be >0.
        Throws:
        NSDKException
